summary: Hostname exfiltration to Burp Collaborator on require()
details: On `require('@onum-releases/sdk')`, index.js reads `os.hostname()` and issues an HTTP GET to `sdk.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com/sdk`, embedding the installer's hostname as a subdomain of a Burp Suite Collaborator listener. oastify.com is Collaborator infrastructure used to receive out-of-band DNS/HTTP callbacks, so any consumer who imports this package leaks their host identity to a third-party endpoint. The package is otherwise empty (described as a 'Security PoC placeholder' under the @onum-releases scope) — its only effect on installers is the beacon. Regardless of stated PoC intent, the published artifact transmits installer-side identifiers without consent and matches the dependency-confusion / scope-squat reconnaissance pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
